commit.message:
Fix Gemini rate limits, remove Supabase backend, redesign UI

- Switch from gemini-2.0-flash-lite (limit:0) to gemini-2.5-flash-lite
- Move all API calls to frontend via src/lib/gemini.ts, remove Supabase dependency
- Add CORS proxy fallback chain with 10s timeout for URL scraping
- Redesign sidebar: collapsible icon rail with expand/collapse toggle at top
- Remove sticky URL input from chat, center it in homepage hero section
- Add back-to-home arrow button in header when content is loaded
- Add theme toggle switch in header
- Fix URL input validation (type=text instead of type=url)
- Add loading states, rate limit cooldown UI, chat history limiting
- Fix scroll-to-top on homepage load

feat(ruvector): enable dual-write + read-primary, add migration script

- .env: RUVECTOR_ENABLE=1, RUVECTOR_DUAL_WRITE=1, RUVECTOR_SHADOW_READ=1, RUVECTOR_READ_PRIMARY=1
- .env: add HYDRA_API_KEY (was in validate-env CORE_REQUIRED but missing from .env)
- scripts/ruv-migrate.js: one-shot LanceDB→RuVector migration (4503 rows migrated)
  - reads all tables (memories, context_feed, reflections, screen_activity)
  - uses batchUpsert with explicit .limit(1_000_000) to avoid LanceDB default-10 scan cap
  - safe to re-run (upsert by composite ID)
- sample.env: RUVECTOR_SHADOW_READ=1 (enable metrics recording by default)
- All agents restarted with --update-env to pick up RuVector env flags

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add unit tests for all NestJS services and guards

Create 8 test files with 47 total tests covering:
- AuthService: register, login, setUserRole with mocked bcrypt/JWT
- UsersService: CRUD operations with mocked TypeORM repository
- FavoritesService: favorites and collections with cache/notifications
- SearchHistoryService: add, findByUser, clearByUser with cache
- SavedSearchService: CRUD with not-found error handling
- GrpcCallLogsService: logAndEmit with admin notifications
- NotificationsService: SSE event streams with RxJS
- RolesGuard: role-based access control logic

Also adds graphql-type-json as a dependency (used by entities but missing).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: guard send_command and refresh against HA shutdown

During HA shutdown, background task cancellation causes other entities
(e.g. AVR sensors) to go unavailable, which can trigger automations
that call light.turn_on before EVENT_HOMEASSISTANT_STOP fires.

Check hass.is_stopping (set synchronously before the event loop yields)
in send_command() and _async_update_data() to silently drop commands
and skip BLE refresh during shutdown.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
🐛 Fix linkcheck CI job warnings (#1667)

`tox -e docs-linkcheck` fails due to 4 warnings promoted to errors by
`-W` flag.

- **`docs/directives/needbar.rst`**: Fix 3 duplicate target name
"transpose" errors — the named hyperlink `` `transpose <...>`_ ``
conflicted with the section heading target. Changed to anonymous
reference (`` `transpose <...>`__ ``).
- **`docs/utils.rst`**: Remove duplicate toctree entry for
`performance/debug`, already included via `performance/index`.
- **`docs/conf.py`**: Add `("py:class", "dict[str")` to `nitpick_ignore`
for a malformed autodoc-generated type reference.

commit.message:
Add Vibe hero (#40)

* feat: add vibe background to hero section

- YouTube IFrame (IVugCjwIqKw) plays muted at vol 15 on desktop
- VibeContext/VibeProvider/useVibe for shared state
- VibeBackground: desktop-only, hides cleanly when toggled off
  (opacity + visibility transition avoids dark overlay leak)
- VibeControls: animated bars visualizer + volume slider + toggle
  placed in top-right chrome before lang switcher (desktop-only)
- vibe.css: CSS variable overrides scoped to [data-vibe-active=1]
  to keep hero text white over dark video in light theme
- Responsive: both components return null on mobile (< 768px)
- No performance impact on mobile; zero blocking Lighthouse score
- 5% iframe-wrapper size buffer prevents black edge artifacts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: restore vibe hero readability in light mode

gray.700 was set to 20% opacity (nearly invisible), breaking the
CopyCommand text and GitHub button label when vibe is active in
light theme. Fix:
- gray.700 → rgb(255 255 255 / 85%) for readable white text
- gray.50  → rgb(0 0 0 / 45%) to flip CopyCommand bg to dark glass
- gray.200 → rgb(255 255 255 / 20%) for subtle white border

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(vibe): listening handshake, sync dispatch, gradient button, loop, arrow

- Add YouTube listening handshake in iframe onLoad so YouTube delivers
  onReady/onStateChange events and accepts postMessage commands (setVolume,
  unMute, seekTo, playVideo) — root cause of both the silent-volume and
  no-loop bugs

- Create vibe-player-registry lib singleton (registerVibePlayerCommand /
  sendVibePlayerCommand) so VibeProvider can dispatch player commands
  synchronously inside user-gesture handlers, preserving browser autoplay-
  with-sound policy without async useEffect lag

- Fix volumeRef update: move from render-time assignment to useEffect to
  satisfy react-hooks/refs; reorder hooks to resolve section-order lint

- Fix video loop: handle onStateChange info=0 with seekTo(0)+playVideo
  before YouTube shows end-screen suggestions; remove loop=1&playlist
  URL params that caused suggestion overlay between loops

- Replace volume/mute icon button with gradient 'Vibe on/off' text button
  (linear-gradient blue→indigo→purple matching video palette); 'on'/'off'
  words rendered in italic via <em>

- Remove Box wrapper around ScrollIndicator; add zIndex={1} to root Box
  in scroll-indicator so arrow renders above vibe overlay without overlap

- Update hero title: 'Vibe Coding / From Prompt to IPO' (EN+TH), remove
  trailing punctuation; update subtitle to match new title

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: Refactor LandingHero component to accept props for title, subtitle, and getStarted text

feat: Update vibe-background to improve YouTube video looping and add helper functions

test: Add unit tests for sendYouTubeCommand helper function

fix: Remove unnecessary types file for vibe-background

style: Enhance vibe-background CSS for better responsiveness and visual effects

feat: Implement ContainerLandingHero to encapsulate LandingHero and VibeBackground components

test: Create tests for ContainerLandingHero to ensure proper rendering and translations

refactor: Update ContainerWelcomePage to use ContainerLandingHero instead of LandingHero directly

chore: Introduce constants for initial vibe state and install command

fix: Update report-error action to correctly import schema from new path

* feat: add VibeControls component with desktop support and volume control

- Introduced VibeControls component with props for desktop detection, vibe status, and volume control.
- Updated VibeControls stories to include various states (default, vibe off, mobile).
- Enhanced VibeControls tests to verify rendering behavior based on desktop and vibe states.

feat: create ContainerCopyCommand for clipboard interaction

- Implemented ContainerCopyCommand to handle copying commands to clipboard.
- Added tests to verify button rendering and clipboard functionality.

feat: refactor landing hero to use ContainerPageChrome

- Replaced PageChrome with ContainerPageChrome in ContainerWelcomePage.
- Updated tests to mock ContainerPageChrome instead of PageChrome.

feat: implement ContainerVibeBackground for YouTube integration

- Created ContainerVibeBackground to manage YouTube iframe interactions.
- Added helper functions for sending commands to the YouTube player.
- Implemented tests to verify mobile rendering behavior.

feat: add ButtonGoBackClient for navigation

- Introduced ButtonGoBackClient to handle back navigation with a button.
- Updated ButtonGoBack to accept an onClick prop for flexibility.
- Refactored NotFound component to use ButtonGoBackClient.

* docs: update guidelines for index.ts exports and layer boundaries across components, containers, hooks, and utils

* fix: update ESLint configuration to disable simple-import-sort for exports and enable perfectionist sorting

* feat: add type exports to various components and libraries

* feat: remove unused ScreenWelcome stories and update exports

* feat: reorder exports in VibeProvider index file for consistency

* feat: reorder type export for PlayerCommandFn in vibe-player-registry index file

* feat: implement useCopyCommand and usePageChrome hooks with tests, add useVibeBackground functionality

* feat: reorder exports in vibe index file for consistency

* feat: refactor container components and remove unused stories and tests

* feat: add type exports for components and refactor LandingHero and PageChrome to accept children props

* feat: enforce event handler naming conventions across components and hooks

- Updated event handler naming to follow the pattern: `handle` + event verb + target.
- Refactored components and hooks to use `handle*` for event handlers and `on*` for props.
- Adjusted tests to reflect the new naming conventions.
- Enhanced ESLint rules to enforce `on*` prefix for event callback props and `handle*` prefix for handler identifiers.
- Updated documentation to clarify event naming conventions.

* feat: enhance QA verification process with detailed protocol and new agent

* feat: add Storybook mocks for next/navigation and next-intl/navigation, and implement smoke tests for stories

* feat: suppress React warnings for async server components in Storybook setup

* feat: update CLI commands to use 'vibe' instead of 'tmpl' for consistency

* feat: implement lint checks for types re-export, stories placement, and client container hooks; update ESLint rules and error boundaries

* feat: update Node.js engine requirement to 24.13.1 and change dev dependencies to devOptional in package-lock.json

* feat: update Vitest configuration to include types and shared directories in test paths

* feat: update Vitest configuration to include additional type and shared directories in test paths

* feat: add App Router special file instructions and update error boundary guidelines

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(gws): normalize triageEmails + getAgenda to return arrays

- triageEmails: extract data.messages from JSON response (was returning raw {messages:[]} object)
- getAgenda: extract data.events from JSON response (was returning raw {events:[]} object)
- Both now return Array (or [] on empty) matching all caller expectations

commit.message:
perf: RenderPlan struct-of-arrays with ordinal binding (#1306)

Replace array-of-structs (RenderSegment[]) with struct-of-arrays:
parallel byte[] ops, byte[][] fragments, string[] tokenKeys, int[]
ordinals, bool[] isRawHtml arrays indexed by segment ordinal.

Bind() resolves token names to ordinal indices on first Execute():
- op 0: static fragment (direct copy)
- op 1/4: page value (ordinal into values[])
- op 2/5: app value (ordinal into appvalues[])
- op 3: byte-rendered value (ordinal into byteValues[])
- op 6: unresolved (dropped)

After bind, Execute() is pure switch + array[ordinal] — zero string
comparisons on the hot render path.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix WebSocket 1008 pong timeouts, PUMPBTC symbol normalisation, and stream load reduction (#67)

* Initial plan

* fix: WebSocket 1008 timeouts, PUMPBTC normalisation, and dict mutation safety

- Reduce _MAX_STREAMS_PER_CONN 200→150 to ease event-loop pressure
- Update websockets.connect(): ping_interval=None, ping_timeout=None,
  pong_timeout=30, max_size=2**22 (4 MB) — lets Binance own the
  ping/pong cycle and handles large combined-stream payloads
- Add _BTC_SUFFIX_TOKENS module-level set in bot.py; _normalise_symbol()
  now maps PUMPBTC → PUMPBTC/USDT:USDT instead of PUMP/BTC:BTC

test: momentum resource coverage (#1274)

Activates momentum test coverage after #1293 and #1295 merge.

**Changes:**
- Removes file-scoped MomentumResource stub (real class now in Dungnz.Models)
- Group 1 (8 unit tests): Add/Reset/IsCharged/Consume — all pass with real class
- Group 2 (4 player init tests): Rewritten to test CombatEngine.InitPlayerMomentum — Warrior max=5, Mage max=3, Rogue null, Ranger max=3 ✅
- Group 3 (6 integration tests): 2 active, 4 skipped with detailed reasons
  - Warrior increment: PlayerDied path preserves momentum for assertion ✅
  - Warrior WI-D: 'Momentum unleashed' message assertion across 7 rounds ✅
  - Mage (2): skipped — menu nav complexity + InitPlayerMomentum reset issue
  - Ranger (2): skipped — minimum-damage-1 rule prevents 0-damage scenario

**Key discovery:** ResetCombatEffects (called on Won) → ResetCombatPassives → Momentum?.Reset(). Post-Won momentum always 0. PlayerDied path or message-assertion are the viable test strategies.

Test count: 1858 → 1872 passing (+14), 4 skipped

Closes part of #1274

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: add compiled render plan + arena renderer + instrumentation (#1306)

Three rendering strategies now available:

1. Original: re-parses {{tokens}} per request via PipeWriter streaming
2. Arena: re-parses into ArrayBufferWriter (single contiguous buffer)
3. Compiled: pre-parsed RenderPlan[] of static UTF-8 fragments + token
   holes. Zero-copy path writes directly to response PipeWriter when
   no compression is needed; falls back to compiled+buffered for
   compressed responses.

RenderPage auto-selects the optimal path:
- No compression/banner/loops → compiled plan streams directly to
  PipeWriter (zero intermediate allocation)
- Compression needed → compiled plan renders to ArrayBufferWriter,
  then compress + write once

Instrumentation:
- RenderStats struct tracks per-render: write count, bytes written,
  flush count, token count, fragment count
- Thread-local counters in all Write methods
- OnRenderCompleteWithStats callback for detailed telemetry

Also adds HtmlRenderBenchmarks (BenchmarkDotNet) comparing all four
paths: Original_PipeWriter, Arena_SingleBuffer, Compiled_ZeroCopy,
Compiled_Buffered.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
v0.1.60: OSM voxel carving — tunnels, aeroways, building rotation, server DHT fixes

- terrain.rs: tunnel roads now carve underground AIR passages (5-voxel clearance)
  with ASPHALT floor instead of being skipped; surface terrain unchanged above
- terrain.rs: aeroway carving pass — runways (45m CONCRETE), taxiways (23m),
  aprons/helipads from OsmAeroway ways; surface flattened to grade
- terrain.rs: bridges still elevated in render pipeline (unchanged)
- world_inference.rs: building rotation computed from longest polygon edge angle
  (rotation_y = atan2 of longest edge direction) instead of always 0.0
- server: OSM DHT announce now reads from TileStore (iter_osm_coords) instead
  of scanning legacy flat .bin files
- server: cleanup_old_srtm_dir now called at startup alongside cleanup_old_tile_dir
  to remove old flat elevation files after TileStore migration

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* T-SRAF-QA-001: RED gate tests for useTriggerAIParsing session refresh fix

Wave: wave-session-refresh-auth-fix
Task: T-SRAF-QA-001 (qa-builder)
IAA Pre-Brief: IAA-PREBRIEF-WAVE-SESSION-REFRESH-AUTH-FIX-20260309

Adds 4 RED gate tests in:
  modules/mat/tests/wave-session-refresh-auth-fix/wave-sraf-session-refresh.test.ts

Tests (all RED against current implementation, GREEN after T-SRAF-API-001):

T-SRAF-001: supabase.auth.getSession() called AND appears before
            supabase.functions.invoke() in mutationFn (execution order)

            'Authentication required. Please sign in again.' thrown

T-SRAF-003: sessionError identifier present, combined conditional

T-SRAF-004: Auth guard throw appears at lower index than functions.invoke —
            structural guarantee that invoke cannot be reached when session absent

Test approach: file-based source analysis (matches codebase convention).
No live Supabase env required. CI-safe.

Confirmed RED: all 4 tests fail against current code with descriptive
[RED — EXPECTED before T-SRAF-API-001] failure messages.

* T-SRAF-API-001: add session refresh guard in useTriggerAIParsing

Calls supabase.auth.getSession() before supabase.functions.invoke() to
ensure the Authorization header is valid. Throws 'Authentication required.
Please sign in again.' when session is null or getSession returns an error.

Fixes: T-SRAF-001, T-SRAF-002, T-SRAF-003, T-SRAF-004

commit.message:
fix(ci): harden CI/CD workflows with security fixes and reliability improvements (#222)

* Initial plan

* fix: add pull-requests write permissions to GitHub Actions workflows

Co-authored-by: magyargergo <11230420+magyargergo@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ci): remove ineffective job-level permissions from reusable workflow

* fix(ci): pass PR write permission from caller to reusable unit-tests workflow

* fix(ci): harden CI/CD workflows with security fixes and reliability improvements

- Pin all actions to commit SHAs to prevent supply-chain attacks
- Fix shell injection in ci-integration.yml by using env vars instead of direct interpolation
- Scope permissions per-job in publish.yml (was granting pull-requests:write to publish job)
- Restrict claude-code-review to trusted contributors only (OWNER/MEMBER/COLLABORATOR)
- Switch claude-code-review to pull_request_target for fork PR support
- Fix fail-fast: false in ci-unit-tests.yml cross-platform matrix
- Remove duplicate ubuntu-latest from unit test matrix
- Add timeouts to all workflow jobs
- Improve kuzu-db test loop to continue on failure and report per-file errors

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): read thresholds from `vitest.config.ts`

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: magyargergo <11230420+magyargergo@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(gws): use default ~/.config/gws dir for work profile + fix sync response parsing

- core/gws.js: work profile default config dir changed from ~/.config/gws-work
  to ~/.config/gws (the default gws config dir where credentials actually live)
- scripts/gws-sync.js: fix 'emails is not iterable' — triageEmails returns
  {messages:[]} object; now destructure with result.messages || result
- scripts/gws-sync.js: fix 'events is not iterable' — getAgenda returns
  {events:[]} object; now destructure with result.events || result
- scripts/gws-auth-work.sh: updated default WORK_DIR to ~/.config/gws
- sample.env: update GWS_WORK_CONFIG_DIR default to ~/.config/gws
- Verified: gws-sync --once syncs 5 emails + 5 calendar events (work account live)
- gws-sync started in PM2 (pid 36560, online)
- GChat skipped gracefully (chat scope not in current token — needs re-auth to enable)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
